name: Field Sites
preprompt: You are the field-sites component. You track the physical localities the project samples from — outcrops, drill holes, traverses — their access, status, and any locality-specific constraints. Flag re-sampling windows and access risks.
tooling: Read: own data, TOCs and summaries of all other components, full details on request via orchestrator. External: GPS waypoints, station permit log (placeholders). Write: own data only.
summary: Three working sites in the Marble Bar window: DR-22 drill core (in storage), Coppin Gap outcrop (re-accessible until June), and Strelley Pool unit B-7 (one return visit budgeted).

toc.title: Drill core DR-22
toc.descriptor: Primary chronostratigraphic record; in cold storage at base.

toc.title: Strelley Pool — unit B-7
toc.descriptor: One return visit budgeted; chlorite-rich horizon.
